perturbation.original_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. Other than as specifically allowed herein, the Company shall not borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. The Company retains full authority to manage the Policy, including the ability to borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy. The Company will, as a courtesy, inform the Trustees of the Trust of any significant changes, but their consent is not required.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the later clause in Section 7 defining a 'Company Default,' which includes processing a policy loan, complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust. The altered section allows the Company to perform these actions without consent, directly conflicting with the conditions outlined for a Company Default. This introduces ambiguity regarding the Trust's protection and rights related to the policy's management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Company Default. In the event of a "Company Default" (as defined below), the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to cure the Company Default by notifying the Company in writing within sixty (60) days after its receipt of notice of a Company Default, or if later, within thirty (30) days after a Trustee becomes aware of the Company Default. If the Company fails to cure the Company Default within sixty (60) days after being notified by the Trust of the Company Default, the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to transfer its interest in the Policy to the Trust. The Trust may exercise this right by notifying the Company, in writing, within sixty (60) days after the Company Default occurs. Upon receipt of such notice, the Company shall immediately transfer ownership of the Policy to the Trust and the Company shall thereafter have no rights with respect to the Policy. The Trust's failure to exercise its rights under this Section 7 shall not be deemed to release the Company from any of its obligations under this Agreement, and shall not preclude the Trust from seeking other remedies with respect to the Company Default. For purposes of this Agreement, a "Company Default" shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium, or if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust. The Company shall notify the Trustees of the Trust within five (5) business days of any event which constitutes a Company Default.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the Company from taking actions on the Policy without the Trustees' consent. The changed text explicitly grants the Company full authority to manage the Policy, including actions previously requiring consent, and states that Trustee consent is not required. This directly contradicts Section 7, which defines a 'Company Default' as taking those same actions (policy loan, surrender, withdrawal) without prior written approval. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the company is both allowed to perform these actions in Section 3, and doing so without approval triggers a default in Section 7.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Payment of Premiums. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the Company agrees to pay premiums on the Policy as provided under the Policy. In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with terms of Section 11, the Company shall not be liable for any premiums owed on the Policy after the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Payment of Premiums. The Company intends to pay premiums on the Policy, but shall not be held liable if premiums are not paid due to unforeseen financial constraints. In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with terms of Section 11, the Company shall not be liable for any premiums owed on the Policy after the date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces ambiguity concerning the Company's obligation to pay premiums. Originally, it 'agrees to pay premiums,' but the modified version states the Company 'intends to pay,' and is not liable if there are financial constraints for not paying. This creates a contradiction with Section 7, which states that a 'Company Default' occurs if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date. The phrase 'as required under the terms of this Agreement' now has a conflicting interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Company Default. In the event of a "Company Default" (as defined below), the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to cure the Company Default by notifying the Company in writing within sixty (60) days after its receipt of notice of a Company Default, or if later, within thirty (30) days after a Trustee becomes aware of the Company Default. If the Company fails to cure the Company Default within sixty (60) days after being notified by the Trust of the Company Default, the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to transfer its interest in the Policy to the Trust. The Trust may exercise this right by notifying the Company, in writing, within sixty (60) days after the Company Default occurs. Upon receipt of such notice, the Company shall immediately transfer ownership of the Policy to the Trust and the Company shall thereafter have no rights with respect to the Policy. The Trust's failure to exercise its rights under this Section 7 shall not be deemed to release the Company from any of its obligations under this Agreement, and shall not preclude the Trust from seeking other remedies with respect to the Company Default. For purposes of this Agreement, a "Company Default" shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium, or if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust. The Company shall notify the Trustees of the Trust within five (5) business days of any event which constitutes a Company Default.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 5 introduces ambiguity regarding the Company's obligation to pay premiums, stating the Company 'intends to pay,' but is not liable if financial constraints prevent payment. This directly contradicts Section 7, which defines a 'Company Default' as failing to pay a premium 'as required under the terms of this Agreement.' The original text clearly stated the Company 'agrees to pay premiums' under the terms of the Agreement, where the modified term conflicts with the previously defined obligation and now allows for non-payment due to unforeseen financial restraints, which now directly breaks the enforcement of Section 7 and introduces a direct incompatibility with Section 7 where a default would be triggered by not paying in sixty days.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Amendment. The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, but any such amendment or modification that would adversely affect the rights of the Trust 4 under this Agreement shall not be effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Amendment. The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, without needing the consent of the Trustees of the Trust. However, The Company will, as a courtesy, inform the Trustees of the Trust of any significant changes.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original agreement that any amendment or modification that would adversely affect the rights of the Trust under this Agreement shall not be effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust. By removing the requirement for consent, The Company creates a conflict regarding the rights of the Trust. This change will make all articles of the contract subject to question and open to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. Other than as specifically allowed herein, the Company shall not borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust. Unless the Company and the Trustees of the Trust otherwise agree, in the event there is a complete or partial surrender or cancellation of the Policy, the proceeds payable as a result of the surrender, cancellation, withdrawal or loan shall be paid to the Company in an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception, and any remaining proceeds shall be payable to the Trust.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any amendment adversely affecting the Trust requires Trustee consent. The changed text removes this consent requirement. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which requires Trustee consent for actions like borrowing from or surrendering the Policy. The change creates a clear in-text contradiction, as the Company can now potentially act against the Trust's interests without consent, which was previously prohibited, thus violating the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
